summary: Source-rights source fidelity is acceptable for the D+Q bone rights seed. The ClinicalTrials.gov source is correctly classified as public_registry for registry payload retention and manifest export; the PubMed-indexed Nature Medicine article is correctly kept metadata_only until a reusable full-text or repository source is separately classified.

findings.claim_or_issue: The ClinicalTrials.gov record is classified as public_registry with raw payload, normalized markdown, section index, structured extraction, and artifact manifest permissions.
findings.why_it_matters: This gives agents a safe first source class for retained text artifacts and repeated extraction without overextending article full-text rights.
findings.recommended_action: Use the registry source-rights record for the first text_snapshot ingestion pass.

findings.claim_or_issue: The PubMed/publisher article source is classified as metadata_only rather than reusable full text.
findings.why_it_matters: This prevents the system from treating article citation or abstract access as permission to retain publisher full text or normalized article markdown.
findings.recommended_action: Create a separate open_reusable or author_manuscript_or_preprint_repository source-rights record only if a qualifying full-text source is found.

findings.claim_or_issue: Both source-rights records include public-export and remediation policy.
findings.why_it_matters: Agentic text ingestion and export repair need explicit machine-readable actions when rights classification changes.
findings.recommended_action: Keep public exports limited to metadata, structured facts, hashes, locators, rights metadata, and artifact manifests unless a rights record explicitly allows retained artifacts.
